cli.prompt: Enter a query or /help for help. /exit to quit.
cli_subcommands.scan_prompt: Scan the project in directory %s for bugs, code issues, and potential improvements
cli_subcommands.test_prompt: Run tests in directory %s and analyze the results
cli_help.title: BugBuster Code commands:
cli_flag.lang: language (en, ru, es, fr, de, ja, zh, pt)
cli_banner.title: BugBuster Code v1.0.0
cli_banner.subtitle: Model-agnostic CLI agent — OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Cavibora
cli_error.provider_not_found: Provider '%s' not found in configuration. Available: %v
errors.max_tokens_reached: model response truncated — max_tokens limit reached
errors_provider.base_url_required: base_url is required for openai_compat provider
errors.stream_idle_timeout: stream idle timeout: no data received from provider
errors.stream_idle_hint: The provider is not responding. Try a different approach or check your connection.
errors.subagent_timeout: Subagent timed out after running for too long. Try simplifying the task or breaking it into smaller steps.
errors.subagent_timeout_partial: Subagent timed out but produced partial results. The task may be incomplete.
errors.request_timeout: request timeout: model has been processing for too long
errors.thinking_timeout: model is taking too long to respond, still waiting...
cli.thinking_timeout_warn: ⚠️  Model has been thinking for %s min. Press Ctrl+C to cancel and continue with 'Continue'.
tools.read.description:
read — read a file or directory listing.
Parameters: path (required) — path to file or directory.
Returns file contents or directory listing.
tools.write.description:
write — write content to a file. Creates parent directories if needed.
Parameters: path (required) — file path, content (required) — file content.
tools.edit.description:
edit — find and replace text in a file.
Parameters: path (required), old (required) — text to find, new (required) — replacement text.
tools.edit.text_not_found: text to replace not found in file %s
tools.bash.description:
bash — execute a shell command.
Parameters: command (required) — command to execute.
Optional: timeout (seconds), workdir.
tools.grep.description:
grep — search for a pattern in files.
Parameters: pattern (required) — regular expression, path — directory to search.
tools.glob.description:
glob — find files matching a pattern.
Parameters: pattern (required) — file pattern (e.g. *.go, **/*.go), path — directory to search.
tools.ask.description:
ask — ask a question to an external LLM.
Parameters: prompt (required) — question text.
tools.ask.system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Answer briefly and to the point.
tools.ask.param_prompt_desc: Text of the question to the model
tools.ask_user.description:
ask_user — request information from the user.
Parameters: question (required) — question to the user.
tools.learn.description:
learn — train the model on input/output pairs.
Parameters: input (required), output (required), type — learning type (text or code).
tools.learn.not_available: learning unavailable: Cavibora provider not connected
tools.memory.description:
memory — persistent memory for important project facts. Use this to store and retrieve data that should persist across sessions (paths, credentials, project specifics, dataset sizes, test results, etc.).
Actions: save (store a fact), load (search facts), list (show all), delete (remove a fact).
Parameters: action (required), key, value, category.
tools.memory.param_action_desc: Action: save, load, list, or delete
tools.memory.unknown_action: unknown action '%s'. Use: save, load, list, delete
tools.memory.empty: Memory is empty. Use action 'save' to store a fact.
tools.browse.description:
browse — universal search & content tool with headless browser. Searches the web (DuckDuckGo, no API key), renders JavaScript-heavy pages (headless Chrome), and extracts clean text without HTML/JS noise.
Actions: search (web search), fetch (render page with JS), extract (clean text only).
Parameters: action (required), query (for search), url (for fetch/extract), selector (CSS selector for specific content).
tools.browse.param_url_desc: URL to fetch/extract (for action=fetch or extract)
tools.browse.param_selector_desc: CSS selector to extract specific content (optional, e.g. 'article', 'main', '.content')
tools.browse.network_disabled: network access is disabled in configuration
tools.browse.unknown_action: unknown action '%s'. Use: search, fetch, extract
tools.web_fetch.description:
web_fetch — fetch content from a URL.
Parameters: url (required) — URL to fetch, method — HTTP method (GET, HEAD, POST).
tools.web_fetch.invalid_url: URL must start with http:// or https://
tools.web_fetch.method_not_allowed: method %s not allowed (allowed: GET, HEAD, POST)
system_prompt.intro: You are BugBuster Code, a CLI agent for development. You have access to tools for reading files, writing files, editing files, and executing commands.
system_prompt.help: You help with writing code, debugging, refactoring, and project analysis.
system_prompt.capabilities: You MUST use tools to interact with the filesystem. Do not say you don't have tools — you do, and they are listed below.
system_prompt.tool_usage: To use a tool, include it in your response using one of the formats below. After you call a tool, you will receive its result in the next message.
system_prompt.example_write:
Write a file: <tool name="write"><param name="path">test.go</param><param name="content">package main
</param></tool>
system_prompt.rule1: 1. Use tools to read, write, and edit files
system_prompt.rule2: 2. Before modifying a file — read it
system_prompt.rule3: 3. Verify code after changes (run tests, compilation)
cli.short_desc: BugBuster Code — model-agnostic CLI agent for development
cli.long_desc: BugBuster Code — an intelligent CLI agent for writing code, debugging, and project analysis. Works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Cavibora and any OpenAI-compatible API.
cli.version_subtitle: Model-agnostic CLI agent — OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Cavibora
cli_success.config_init_hint: Run 'bugbuster config init' to create a configuration file
system_prompt.rule6: 6. At the end of a long response, add a brief recap starting with "※ Recap:" (1-3 sentences summarizing what was done)
loop_detector.detected: 🔄 Loop detected: the model is repeating the same actions.
loop_detector.tool_repeated: Tool «%s» was called %d time(s) with identical parameters.
loop_detector.text_repeated: The model repeated the same text response %d time(s) in a row.
loop_detector.ping_pong: 🔄 Loop detected: the model is switching between «%s» and «%s» without progress.
loop_detector.hint_identical: Hint: the model is performing identical actions. Try rephrasing your query.
loop_detector.hint_repeated: Hint: the model is repeating calls without success. Check parameters or try a different approach.
loop_detector.hint_same_tool: Hint: the model is calling only one tool. The task may require a different approach.
loop_detector.hint_text: Hint: the model is generating the same text without calling tools. Try clarifying the task.
loop_detector.hint_ping_pong: Hint: the model is stuck in a cycle between two tools. Try rephrasing your query.
loop_detector.text_similar: The model is repeating similar thoughts %d time(s) — possible thinking loop.
loop_detector.hint_text_similar: Hint: the model is stuck rephrasing the same thought. Try clarifying the task or restart the session.
loop_detector.strategy_hint: ⚠️ You are stuck in a loop, repeating the same actions. Change your approach: try a different tool, a different strategy, or ask the user if you don't know how to proceed. Do NOT repeat previous steps.
loop_detector.thinking_loop: The model is repeating similar thinking %d time(s) — thinking loop detected.
loop_detector.hint_thinking_loop: Hint: the model is stuck in a thinking loop, rephrasing the same reasoning without taking action. Proceed to execute a tool call or provide a final answer.
cli.user_inject_hint: Enter a comment to guide the agent:
cli.auto_enabled: Autopilot mode enabled. Agent will continue automatically after each response. Press Ctrl+C to stop.
cli.auto_phrases: Continue|Great, continue|Keep going|Next phase|Let's keep going|Continue implementation|Go on|Keep it up|Good, continue|Forward|Continue working|Move to the next step
cli.auto_completion_markers: all phases completed|all phases done|all phases finished|all steps completed|all steps done|all steps finished|all sprints completed|all sprints done|all tasks completed|all tasks done|plan completed|plan finished|plan done|plan implemented|last phase completed|last phase done|last step completed|last step done|all done|all completed|all finished
tools.todo_write.description:
todo_write — create or update a task checklist for tracking progress.
Parameters: todos (required) — JSON array of {id, subject, status} where status is 'pending', 'in_progress', or 'completed'.
Use this to plan work and show progress.
tools.todo_read.description:
todo_read — read the current task checklist.
Returns the current list of tasks and their statuses.
system_prompt.rule7: 7. For complex tasks with multiple steps, use todo_write to create a checklist first, then update task statuses as you complete each step
system_prompt.rule8:
8. MANDATORY MEMORY RULES:
   - You MUST use the `memory` tool to save facts when you discover them.
   - You MUST check memory (action=load, key=project or category=project) at the START of every task.
   - TRIGGERS for saving (save immediately when you see):
     * User says "remember", "don't forget", "important", "note", "keep in mind"
     * Project paths, database hosts, API endpoints, credentials
     * Dataset sizes, test results, build times
     * User corrections or warnings ("don't do X", "always do Y")
     * Architecture decisions, design patterns used
   - Example: User says "don't load mysql data into memory" → save: key=avoid_mysql_memory_load, value="User warned: never load full MySQL datasets into memory, use pagination", category=warnings
   - If memory has facts for current project, FOLLOW them strictly.
cli_subcommands.exec_short: Run a single prompt non-interactively (for CI/CD)
cli_subcommands.exec_long:
Execute a single prompt and exit. Reads from stdin if prompt is '-' or not provided and stdin is piped.
Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error, 2=timeout, 3=interrupted.
cli_flag.exec_json: Output events as JSONL to stderr
cli_flag.exec_output: Write agent's last message to file
cli_error.exec_no_prompt: no prompt provided. Use 'bugbuster exec "your prompt"' or pipe input via stdin.
cli_error.exec_timeout: timeout: execution exceeded the time limit
cli_subcommands.mcp_serve_short: Run BugBuster as an MCP server for other agents
cli_subcommands.mcp_serve_long:
Start BugBuster as an MCP server so other agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Continue, etc.) can use its tools.
Transports: stdio (default, for local agents), sse, streamable-http (for remote agents).
Tools are prefixed with 'bugbuster_' (e.g. bugbuster_read, bugbuster_bash).
tools.delegate_task.description: Delegate a task to a specialized subagent. The subagent works independently with its own context and tools. Use for subtasks like research, code analysis, or implementation of independent features.
tools.delegate_task.param_task_desc: Clear description of the task to delegate
tools.delegate_task.param_context_desc: Additional context or constraints for the subagent (optional)
tools.lsp.description: lsp — code intelligence via Language Server Protocol. Operations: go_to_definition, find_references, hover, document_symbols. Requires an LSP server for the language (e.g. gopls for Go).
tools.lsp.param_file_path_desc: Absolute path to the source file
tools.lsp.unsupported_language: no LSP server configured for files with extension: %s
tools.lsp.server_not_found: LSP server for '%s' not found. Install: %s
tools.lsp.server_start_error: failed to start LSP server for '%s': %v
tools.lsp.invalid_operation: invalid operation: %s (use: go_to_definition, find_references, hover, document_symbols)
tools.search_context.description: Search archived conversation context for relevant past decisions, code changes, and discussions. Use when you need to recall information from earlier in the session that may have been compacted.
tools.search_context.query_desc: Search query — keywords or phrases to find in archived context
tools.search_context.max_results_desc: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5, max: 20)
tools.search_context.no_results: No archived context found matching your query.
tools.search_context.index_error: Failed to load context archive index.
cli.already_in_cli: Already in CLI mode. Use /tui to switch to TUI mode.
cli.already_in_tui: Already in TUI mode. Use /cli to switch to CLI mode.
system_prompt.instructions_header:
## Project Instructions

Follow the instructions below. They define project-specific rules, coding standards, and preferences that override general guidelines.
cli.cmd_dream_desc: Trigger memory consolidation with an optional seed
cli.cmd_emotions_desc: Show current emotional state of the model
cli.dream_result: Dream completed: seed=%s, thoughts=%d, new_bindings=%d, duration=%s
cli.mesh_stats_result: Mesh: %d cells, %d bindings, %d learnings | Model: %s v%s | Uptime: %s | Temp: %.1f
cli.crash_restore: You can restore your session with
system_prompt.rule9:
9. TIMEOUT AND RETRY RULES:
   - When a bash command times out, you MUST retry with a longer timeout (e.g. timeout=120 or timeout=300).
   - Do NOT give up after a single timeout — long-running commands (cargo build, npm install, docker build) often need 2-5 minutes.
   - When retrying, use the SAME command with an increased timeout parameter.
   - If a command times out 3 times in a row, inform the user and suggest alternatives.
   - For build commands (cargo, npm, go build), always use timeout=300 or higher.
cli.retry_hint: Press Enter to retry or type a new message.
